name: Lyra Therapeutics, Inc.
business: We have historically been a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the development and commercialization of innovative, anti-inflammatory therapies for the localized treatment of patients with chronic rhinosinusitis (CRS). LYR-210 is a bioabsorbable nasal implant designed to deliver six months of continuous anti-inflammatory drug therapy for CRS. As of January 2026, the company suspended further development of LYR-210 and is pursuing strategic alternatives.
sellsTo: CRS patients who have failed previous medical management

exposures.sentence: We have historically been a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the development and commercialization of innovative, anti-inflammatory therapies for the localized treatment of patients with chronic rhinosinusitis, or CRS.

tamClaims.claim: CRS affects approximately 14 million people in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: CRS is an inflammatory disease of the paranasal sinuses which leads to debilitating symptoms and significant morbidities and affects approximately 14 million people in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Sinusitis impacts approximately 12% of the U.S. adult population, or about 30 million people.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that sinusitis, which includes both CRS and acute rhinosinusitis, impacts approximately 12% of the adult population in the United States, or approximately 30 million people, making it the fifth most common condition in people under the age of 65 and more prevalent than diabetes or heart disease.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 14 million people are affected by CRS.
tamClaims.sentence: Of this population, we estimate that approximately 14 million people are affected by CRS.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 8 million people are treated for CRS annually, of whom 4 million fail medical management every year.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that approximately 8 million people are treated for CRS by physicians annually, of whom approximately 4 million fail medical management every year.
